title: for — the classic
successMessage: Three parts: initialise (let i = 0), condition (i <= 5), update (i++). The loop runs while condition is true.

title: for...of — loop over any iterable

codeChecks.message: Replace the TODO comment with your code.

successMessage: for...of is clean, safe, and works on any iterable. Prefer it over classic for whenever you don't need the index.

title: entries() — index AND value
instruction:
Need both the index and the value while iterating? `.entries()` gives you both as `[index, value]` pairs.

Run this to number the list starting from 1.
successMessage: entries() destructures to [index, value]. Note index + 1 to start numbering at 1 instead of 0. This pattern replaces manual index tracking.

title: for...in — loop over object keys
starter:
const user = { name: "Alex", age: 25, city: "London", role: "admin" };

// TODO: use for...in to print each key: value pair

codeChecks.message: Replace the TODO comment with your code.

successMessage: for...in for objects, for...of for arrays and iterables. Keep that rule in your head — using for...in on arrays gives you string indexes and includes inherited properties.

title: while — loop until something changes
instruction:
`while` keeps going as long as the condition is true. Use it when you don't know upfront how many iterations you need.

⚠️ Always make sure the condition will eventually become false — or you get an infinite loop.

Simulate a dice roll that keeps rolling until you get a 6.
successMessage: do...while always executes at least once — perfect for 'roll until success'. The number of iterations varies every run since it's random.

title: break — exit early
instruction:
`break` immediately exits the loop — even if there are items remaining.

Perfect for search: once you find what you want, stop. No point checking the rest.

Search for the first premium user and break when found.

codeChecks.message: Use break to exit the loop early.
Replace the TODO comment with your code.

successMessage: David was never checked. break saved the work. In a database with millions of users, finding on record 3 and breaking is essential for performance.

title: continue — skip this one
instruction:
`continue` skips the rest of the current iteration and jumps to the next one.

`break` = stop the loop entirely.
`continue` = skip this item, keep going.

Print only active users (skip inactive ones).

codeChecks.message: Use continue to skip inactive users.
Replace the TODO comment with your code.

successMessage: Bob and David skipped. Alice, Carol, Eve printed. continue says 'skip the rest of this iteration' — the loop keeps going.

title: forEach — simplest array loop

codeChecks.message: Replace the TODO comment with your code.

successMessage: forEach is clean and readable for simple iteration. But remember: no break inside forEach. If you need to stop early, for...of is the right choice.

title: Nested loops — with care
instruction:
Loops inside loops. The inner loop runs completely for every outer iteration.

⚠️ 10 outer × 10 inner = 100 iterations. 1000 × 1000 = 1,000,000. Nested loops scale quadratically — be careful with large data.

Print a 3×3 multiplication table.

codeChecks.message: Use nested loops — a loop inside a loop.
Replace the TODO comment with your code.

successMessage: 9 outputs, 5 lines of code. Nested loops are powerful for tables, matrices, and pair comparisons — just keep an eye on the scale.

title: FizzBuzz — the classic interview question
instruction:
Almost every programming interview has this. You now have everything you need.

Rules:
- Print 1 to 30
- Divisible by 3: print `Fizz`
- Divisible by 5: print `Buzz`  
- Divisible by both: print `FizzBuzz`

`n % 3 === 0` checks divisibility. Check FizzBuzz first or it can never win.
hint: Check % 15 (both) first, then % 3, then % 5, then the number.

codeChecks.message: Replace the TODO comment with your code.

successMessage: FizzBuzz done! This question filters out a huge percentage of interview candidates. You just nailed it. Chapter 6 complete — you can loop anything now.
